reviews.title: Review service เรื่องอาหารและบริการ
reviews.text:
The level of responsibility in food preparation at this hotel was extremely disappointing.

We ordered the same menu item at the pool bar on two consecutive days: Spaghetti Carbonara.
However, we were served penne pasta instead — on both days.

We specifically asked whether the dish included egg. The staff confirmed that it did, so we ordered an extra egg as well.
In the end, there was no egg in the dish at all.

On the second day, we asked again before ordering if the dish contained egg. The staff confirmed that it did. We ordered an additional egg again.
Once more, the dish arrived without egg — and we were charged for two extra eggs on the bill.

This happened two days in a row.
The food was incorrect both times, yet there was no apology, no compensation, and no sense of responsibility. We still had to pay the full amount.

In addition:
	•	Guests are not allowed to take food to eat elsewhere.
	•	Outside delivery is not permitted.
	•	The hotel claims there is no space for deliveries, which is quite unacceptable for a five-star hotel.

Overall, the staff training does not reflect five-star standards.
The service was not worth the price paid.

For those paying by credit card, I strongly recommend checking your bill carefully. There may be billing errors.

In our case, we paid the deposit in cash, which allowed us to notice several issues.
Even though this is advertised as a five-star hotel, honesty and service standards were very disappointing.

reviews.title: The booking issue via booking.com has completely ruined the mood of my vacation
reviews.positives: Having a comprehensive facilities
reviews.negatives:
I am giving this rating specifically due to a major booking discrepancy caused by Booking.com. I booked a room for 5 guests and paid a higher price to include an extra bed. However, Booking.com provided incorrect information to the hotel, claiming I had only booked for 4 people.

Despite my confirmation clearly showing 5 guests, the hotel could not provide the extra bed without an additional charge because of the misinformation from Booking.com's customer service. I ended up being double-charged for the same service. This was an incredibly frustrating experience.

I will never book through Booking.com again. Their lack of accountability and misinformation led to this mess. I strongly advise everyone to double-check their guest count with the hotel directly or avoid using Booking.com altogether

reviews.title: Disappointing Stay – Would Not Recommend
reviews.text:
I booked a room through Agoda for 3 adults, including my daughter, but when we arrived, the hotel insisted on charging us extra even though the booking was clearly for 3 people and we were using the existing beds.

The hotel was noisy every morning with kids screaming – definitely not a relaxing resort if you’re looking for peace and quiet. Housekeeping repeatedly forgot to provide extra towels and toiletries for the third person, despite us paying extra.

To make things worse, on the day we checked out, we had an issue with our Grab transportation to Bangkok, and when we asked staff for help, they refused to assist and didn’t want to get involved at all.

Overall, it was a very disappointing experience. The staff were unfriendly and unhelpful, and I would not recommend this hotel if you’re looking for a relaxing or welcoming stay.

reviews.title: “Beautiful hotel, but not well maintained property”
reviews.text:
Rooms are spacious, nicely furnished but not maintained well. In our room (4th floor), stained tiles on the floor, dirty marks on the door, finger prints on the mirror bathroom frame, dirt lines around the shower window and there was mold on the walls. Also if you leave the bathroom door open, you could hear water running from the room above, somewhat annoying. 
Public areas standards are well below what I’d call acceptable, corridors and elevators were dirty.  
Well, for the breakfast, quite variety and tasty but need to improve rather than let guest waiting for a table. Crowded breakfast at Amber kitchen, the dining room needs a serious rethinking, as it is almost impossible to move around in there and took a while to get a seat. The pool area was lovely and the pool bar staff were great. 
Overall, it is such a pity that such a nice hotel (location & building wise) is not maintained properly. I hope the management can look seriously into this.

reviews.title: Nice resort but service and food were disappointing
reviews.text:
Arrived at 2pm and there were no bellmen to help with bags. After driving 3 hours from Bangkok you expect their to be someone to help take bags out of the car and help with parking and check in. Staff must have been busy and were not there so we needed to do it ourself. Then once we got to check in were told room was still not ready. Went to eat some lunch and the pool bar and they were out of the food we ordered. Bartenders were really rude. All in all it was a terrible check in and first experience. 

When we finally got to the the room it was very nice. Hotel staff seemed unable to deal with lots of guests at the restaurants and we saw many other tables unhappy with service. Overall not up to normal Marriott service standards. 

This hotel is new and trending now and when hotels and restaurants get very busy in thailand they usually can't keep up and service suffers. We will go back to our usual resort that is a little older but where the staff are always great till the Marriott improves.

reviews.title: Not a 5 Star Hotel
reviews.text:
I was aware that this hotel is brand new and I never write reviews. Let me first say that I live in Thailand and am aware of the differences in customer service compared to American hotels, so I believe this is a pretty accurate review. 
Issues:
1-We checked in at 11 and were told our room was not ready. We were told we would be called when it was. We never received a call and finally, at 2pm, we went to ask and were told it had been ready for a while.
2-Our friends' boy cracked his chin on the pool side and were given a piece of cake. The wide pool slide is dangerous.
3-Our room was like a cave. Low ceilings and very small  for a 5 star. I asked for another room and was told that there were no rooms because there was a corporate Mariott party going on there. 
4-For a 5 Star hotel, the grounds were lovely, but there was no bathtub in the room and the room/bath was tiny.
5-There was no wifi in our building and the TV channels went out often. 
6-The public toilet in the lobby was in the basement parking garage.
7-The breakfast looked amazing, but it was atrocious. We had to wait 30 minutes for a table. They were out of may items and it was messy because it was so busy. We were told that many rooms had not been finished yet. There is no way they can accommodate that many people for breakfast. We finally left and went for breakfast elsewhere. 
8-It took over 45 minutes to check out and we were charged 1100 baht from the mini bar. Our driver was waiting 30 minutes so when the staff asked me if I wanted and itemized list, I was afraid it would take another 45 minutes, so I just paid it.
9-The staff was nice. I got the feeling they were just embarrassed at what a disorganized mess the hotel was. 

This hotel needed to stay closed for at least another month before opening to train the staff and work out all of the problems. This was very disappointing as I live in Bangkok and am always recommending resorts to colleagues and friends.

reviews.title: Disappointed Stay – Not Ideal for a Quiet Getaway ⭐️⭐️☆☆☆
reviews.text:
The staff was friendly and welcoming at check-in, which gave us high hopes. Unfortunately, the room itself was a big letdown. It felt outdated, and the blanket had a strange odor that lingered even after we tried using room spray — it was hard to ignore.

The biggest issue, however, was the noise. If you’re looking for a peaceful and relaxing stay, this is not the place. The hotel was extremely noisy throughout our visit. Every morning and all day long, we had to deal with the constant sound of children screaming, running, and playing. It was absolutely unbearable at times.

The room had very poor sound insulation — we could hear everything happening in the hallways and even from other rooms. It made it very difficult to rest or enjoy any quiet time.

Overall, we were disappointed and wouldn’t recommend this hotel to anyone looking for a calm, restful experience in Hua Hin.

reviews.text:
A lovely resort very well maintained with charming efficient staff. 
However there are far too many rooms at the resort for the available facilities. We were unable to sit at the adult pool as there is a limited number of seats. There are other pools but these are aimed at families and naturally less tranquil. 
Similarly, although there is a private beach area there are only a small number of sun loungers and there is no shade available on the beach. 
In summary, I would say stay here as a family with children, it is ideal but if you are paying a premium price for a restful break in quiet surroundings and have high expectations of the facilities you may be disappointed.

reviews.title: A very enjoyable and comfortable stay though we occasionally felt the peak occupancy
reviews.positives: The hotel is well-located on the main street (walking distance to shops and restaurants and the night market) and on a nice stretch of beach. The common areas and rooms are nicely decorated, well-maintained and very clean while the landscaping and multiple pools are picturesque. The housekeeping service was great, very prompt and helpful and many restaurant and reception staff members were pleasant and keen to help despite their high volume of work. I appreciated the adults only pool for the quieter, more relaxed vibe vs. the fun, family-friendly pool areas. I also liked the free workout classes ranging from yoga and tai chi to core workouts and kickboxing.
reviews.negatives:
We visited over new year's eve which is likely the hotel's peak occupancy period, yet I expect the Marriott to account for the same and maintain its high standard of service. When visiting the beach bar for a meal, I was standing at the maitre'd station for nearly 10 minutes in front of many staff members including an F&B manager until I approached a waiter and asked to be seated, then waited another 10 minutes before getting up to flag someone down to take my order. There were only about 10 deck chairs on the beach and never any staff member to set up more chairs, provide towels or take F&B orders, which was unusual vs. every other beach resort I've visited. 

FYI: There are lots of families as the hotel has nice kids facilities and kids pools. It can get a bit noisy, though not loud enough to bother me, especially as there is an adults-only pool.

reviews.title: Good hotel. A few teething problems.
reviews.text:
We stayed over Easter and were encouraged by staff (Nancy) to upgrade from breakfast to Easter Sunday brunch. This was impeccable.

Our kids had a great time swimming around the resort from our pool access room. This is only worth doing if your child is a competent swimmer. The route is waist deep for adults.

The pirate ship playground, waterpark and kids club made sure we had plenty of adult time. This is good place for families.

The hotel is new and this shows I'm the lack of shade- it's all very exposed compared to more established hotels. Beds are comfy and it's all very clean.

There are a lot of Mosquitos. Take repellent.

Checkout was a real headache- we Checked in and out twice during our trip and on both occasions it took a very long time- we had to dispute bills and discounts (including champagne we were offered 'on the house' which turned up on the bill) and we ended up moving out own luggage to save time. It's hard to manage cranky tired kids in a hotel lobby for long periods of time.

I hope the Marriot can sort out these teething difficulties soon.
reviews.positives: Easter Sunday brunch- excellent. Pool access allows you to swim around much of the venue. Great kids pirate ship waterpark playground

At the checking out day, we were called from the front desk at almost 1pm asking whether we check out today or not. I said yes we are, at 2pm as agreed. The front desk then told us that this was impossible, no record of late check out offered. Then, the front desk asked us to check out immediatley by 1.30pm (feel like we were kicked out) Wheather this was a mistake between the staff or whatever, the 30 minutes (1.30 pm and 2 pm) does not  make any difference for the hotel but really it make the loyal customers very disappointed. So, we recommend visitors to ask the front desk officer to write it down for you if they offer you the late check out upon cheking in process.

reviews.title: 5 star lifestyle -
reviews.text:
My booking was for 2 Superior rooms to accommodate a family of 4 on a week long holiday. We requested connecting rooms and in our communications with the hotel via Agoda's property chat feature, were led to believe that this was possible. However, during check-in, we found out that this setup wasn't available for the first 4 nights of our stay. We ended up paying for an upgrade to a Junior suite with 2 bedrooms and a living room for those 4 nights, so this review will be for the Junior Suite room type.

As a resort, Marriott Hua Hin delivers on everything you need in terms of 5 star living.  Firstly on the dining options: The daily breakfast buffet was of a really high standard and effectively covered any guests regardless of food requirements. Do note that buffet service gets super busy during the weekends so service gets hectic and isn't as seamless as it was during the week. Our favourite on-site dining option was the Big Fish, and we enjoyed both the lunch and dinner experience. For those with kids, I'd say lunch at Big Fish is probably the best in terms of value and quality, plus you get to see the beach from air-conditioned comfort. For those looking for a solid coffee hit, the resort has an on-site bakery that does light bites.

Room wise, the Junior Suite was very spacious and came with a jacuzzi that was heated. Our room was also in the building closest to the Kids Club so that was extremely convenient for drop-offs/pick-ups of the kids. If there was any feedback, it was that the housekeeping team didn't realise that different guests had moved into the room so we had a welcome message created for the previous guest left on the mirror of the hallway for the duration of our stay. 

There were 3 pools (main, smaller one for kids with a slide, and loop pool) for enjoyment. It was well maintained though I wish there were more complimentary inflatables or noodle floats from Marriott for the loop pool.

In terms of location, the hotel is situated between the main shopping mall in Hua Hin and the Old town's night market and it's easily accessible by car. Do note that the road traffic does build up during evening rush hour if you're planning to check out the local nightlife. That aside, the area along the main road isn't really designed for pedestrian walking even if you are game for it. This is in contrast to the Hilton or Centara which are connected to a neighbourhood with lively bars and shops on the streets, and much closer to the Old Town.

reviews.title: One of Marriott’s Greatest
reviews.text:
It’s been a while since the last time we stayed in Marriott chain, and this last one surely one of best experiences in the chain.

Pros;
Relatively new property with proper design, good landscape, good sized room,  comfy beds, nice gestures  and hospitality from hotel members, location is good and close to major attractions.

Cons;
Lighting in the room could be better, stayed in #5109 (if not mistaken), a one bedroom junior suite. Only one switch for many lights in the living, so it’s either all is on which really bright, or all offs full dark, similar lighting mode at the bedroom.

Cleanliness issue inside the elevators in our building, not a 5 stars standard. Felt and smells filthy which apparently caused by hot humid weather plus high usage from many guests, must clean more properly and frequently so they are good as the ones at the main building where the grand lobby sits.

The beach is beautiful, but you should put the horses away, their bladders seen everywhere.

reviews.title: Best resort I've stayed in!
reviews.text:
My stay at the Marriott Huahin was truly exceptional, marking it as the finest resort experience I've ever had the pleasure of enjoying. The resort's five pools, intricately interconnected in a delightful loop, provided an unparalleled aquatic retreat. During happy hour, the meticulously crafted cocktails added an extra layer of joy to my evenings.

The view from my room was nothing short of breathtaking, offering a picturesque panorama that added a touch of magic to each moment. The convenience of beach access further enhanced the overall experience, allowing me to seamlessly transition from the luxurious amenities of the resort to the pristine sandy shores.

One of the highlights of my stay was indulging in a sumptuous breakfast at Big Fish, where the view of the ocean served as the perfect backdrop to a truly delectable meal. The culinary delights, combined with the soothing sounds of the waves, created a memorable dining experience that lingered in my thoughts long after my departure.

In conclusion, I wholeheartedly recommend the Marriott Huahin to anyone seeking a beach resort getaway. The harmonious blend of luxurious amenities, stunning views, and impeccable service make it an unparalleled choice for an unforgettable retreat in Huahin.

reviews.title: Check your bill!!!!
reviews.text: We have stayed at the Hua Hin Marriott about  6-7 times over the years since it opened.  It's a great place to relax, especially with the separate adult pool.  What I didn't like, however, was the fact that as we checked out yesterday I decided to check the bill, which I normally don't do.  And there it was - a breakfast bill for about 2,800 baht that did not belong to me.  I asked to check the signed receipt, and the room number was mine but the signature and name was not.  Of course the staff member removed it from my bill, but for a hotel in this price range that's not an acceptable mistake.  When I stay at cheaper hotels, yeah sure the lowly paid staff try to sneak in a receipt at check out.  But I should not have to deal with something like this at a hotel I am paying nearly $300 per night!

reviews.title: Perfect Escape From Busy World
reviews.text:
Located in the heart of Hua Hin, Marriott is a place that you can fully forget about the busy world and just relax. The standard is beyond exceptional, with helpful staffs and large variety of food zone to dine. The cafe was beautifully designed with relaxing atmosphere, and the poolside was fantastic.

Near the hotel there's a large supermarket within the walking distance as well.

Will definitely visit again.

reviews.title: Lovely Stay
reviews.text: Rarely can you go wrong staying in a Marriott.  Staff, breakfast, cleaners, and room were all great. My primary complaint is that the adult pool doesn't open until 9am. I love an early morning lap swim for exercise. I understand why the other pools open late, but it doesn't make sense to not allow an adult to quietly swim laps in the adult-only pool. Also, the cleaning crews work on the pools at 9am. This should be done when the pool is closed the previous 14 hours. Also, why are there only 6 beach chairs? Strange for such a nice hotel on such a nice stretch of beach.

reviews.text:
We stayed in a poolside room for two nights. 
The staff at the front desk were friendly and offered drinks and balloons to the kids.
The room had direct access to the pool, which was perfect for kids, but the pool hours were short, and kids had to wait until 9 am to swim. The lazy river pool was fun, and there are also waterslide.
It was very thoughtful that when we went to the family pool, a staff member kindly brought us towels and set up beach chairs for us. They saw that our inflatable pool toys weren't fully inflated and asked if we needed help, and they used a pump to inflate them for us.
There were popsicles served every afternoon, and there was also a cotton candy station, which the kids loved.

We were looking forward to the kids club, but the activities were a bit monotonous and didn't attract the kids. We hope they could add a playhouse or playground.

reviews.title: Nice hotel for families and couples with friendly staff
reviews.text: The Hotel including the staff is well organized and prepared for the customer. Very friendly and warm welcome including parking service (charging for EV cars was available as well).  The rooms are bright, big enough for 2 adults and 2 kids. Very modern and clean. Big TV and a mini bar is available. Outside you can find two restaurants, one of them directly on the beach. The pizza I cannot recommend. For the kids are two slides available including a water park. There is a separate swimming pool only for adults. So this hotel is prepared for families but as well for singles or couples. Our kids had a lot of fun in the swimming pool but also on the slides. The kids club I can recommend, very friendly staff and the kids had fun. In general a very great and high class Hotel.

reviews.title: One of the best hotel We’ve experienced.😃
reviews.text:
The staffs are awesome. Food are exceptional. Location is perfect. And facilities are great. 

The only 2 disturbing elements we think if they are improved, we’ll extremely satisfied.

1. Hotel door guard. 
As 6 stars, we quite expected him to recognize our vehicle after we’ve checked in and drive in&out the hotel area twice. He kept asking when entering to the hotel area. We understood this is for our own safety but we just believe for Marriott, he can do better. Asking every single entering was just not satisfied.

2. The balcony door 
It needs to be shut at all time otherwise the air-con will get turned off. We sometimes just would like to stay on bed and absorb sea breeze, beach view and wave sound with air con on. Anyway, we totally understand this is for energy saving.

reviews.title: Fantastic stay full of great memories. A+ to the staff. We'll be back!
reviews.positives:
First of all, the staff here are incredible and really make the experience. They're always smiling, warm and friendly. Literally everyone on the property makes an effort to say hi and greet you with a smile. Every request we made to the front desk was greeted with a quick, cheery, "yes!" followed by prompt service.

Everything about the resort, physically, was excellent. The rooms are new and modern yet very comfy and cozy. The food was great everywhere we ate. Pools are beautiful and the beach access is perfectly situated. It's got a nicely equipped gym and the lobby has great lounge vibes for evening cocktails before heading out.
reviews.negatives:
Biggest thing by far is the very distasteful management decision to exclude a whopping 17.5% from every listed price in taxes, fees, and service charges. It was a stain on our experience in this otherwise wonderful place. 10% service charges at literally every restaurant on Earth are a plague already, but at an expensive 5-star resort, seeing every bill come back marked up nearly 20% from listed prices was insulting. The price should be the price. Slipping in (especially this much) extra is plainly unethical, it's a really bad look, and it's ultimately low-wage service workers who suffer from lost tips because of it (though we made sure to still take care of everyone).

For some reason, as a couple, we were put in a room right next to the kiddie pool, but thankfully the hotel was proactive in ensuring it didn't open until 9am, so we weren't bothered by it. On the plus side it was also very near the beach and adult pool as well.

reviews.title: Best for family or kid's friendly Hotel
reviews.text:
I had a wonderful stay at the Marriott Hua Hin Resort & Spa. The room was clean, comfortable, and very quiet, which made it perfect for a relaxing family trip. The staff were extremely polite, friendly, and always ready to help.

I booked a family room, which was actually two standard rooms connected by a door. It worked perfectly for us, and my two kids absolutely loved having their own space. Our first impression was great—the hotel welcomed the kids with warm chocolate ball snacks, which was a lovely touch.

The resort offers many activities for children. The highlight is definitely the swimming pool, especially the large white slide. The pool depth felt safe for my 7-year-old, which gave me peace of mind.

I also really enjoyed the beach area. There’s a horse riding service available (around 400 THB per ride), and my kids had a great time with it. Personally, my favorite moment was during low tide. I loved walking along the beach and exploring—especially finding hermit crabs, which I couldn’t easily find on beaches like Pattaya or Koh Samet. That made Hua Hin beach feel very special to me.

The food was excellent with nothing to complain about. There was a wide variety of options, including Thai, European, Chinese dishes, and a fresh salad bar.

Overall, if you are looking for a kid-friendly hotel in Hua Hin, I highly recommend this place.

Cheers!
